title: Cusco 7-Day Bucket List Trip
metaTitle: Cusco 7-Day Bucket List Trip | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 7-day trip to Cusco, Peru: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Machu Picchu and Sacsayhuamán. Prices from $1124 per person (estimate).
highlights: Trekking the classic Inca Trail to Machu Picchu
overview: Spend 7 days discovering Cusco, Peru — High-altitude (~3,400m) mountain climate with cool days and cold nights year-round (highs around 19-20°C, dropping toward 17-18°C in the wetter months) and a pronounced dry season (May-September) versus wet season (December-March) with big diurnal temperature swings. May to September is the dry season with the clearest trail and mountain conditions, though also the busiest and coolest window (chilly nights); shoulder months (April, October) offer fewer crowds. This itinerary blends Machu Picchu, Sacsayhuamán, Plaza de Armas, Cusco with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
6 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Machu Picchu, Sacsayhuamán, Plaza de Armas, Cusco
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Cusco
itinerary.description: Land at Alejandro Velasco Astete International Airport (CUZ) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Machu Picchu — The iconic 15th-century Inca citadel, Peru's premier bucket-list site. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Cusco: Sacsayhuamán & Qorikancha (Temple of the Sun) and Santo Domingo Convent
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Sacsayhuamán — Massive Inca fortress with megalithic zigzag stone walls overlooking Cusco. In the afternoon, continue to Qorikancha (Temple of the Sun) and Santo Domingo Convent — Inca temple foundations beneath a colonial church. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Qorikancha (Temple of the Sun) and Santo Domingo Convent

itinerary.title: Cusco: Sacred Valley full-day tour (Pisac, Ollantaytambo) & Rainbow Mountain (Vinicunca) day trek
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Sacred Valley full-day tour (Pisac, Ollantaytambo) — Guided tour of Inca ruins and markets in the Sacred Valley. In the afternoon, continue to Rainbow Mountain (Vinicunca) day trek — Striking multicolored mountain at over 5,000m elevation. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Machu Picchu (via Ollantaytambo train)
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Machu Picchu (via Ollantaytambo train), a popular side trip from Cusco. Return to Cusco in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Machu Picchu (via Ollantaytambo train)

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Humantay Lake glacial hike — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Cusco: Cusco Cathedral & Museo Inka
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Cusco Cathedral — Grand colonial cathedral on the Plaza de Armas, built over an Inca palace. In the afternoon, continue to Museo Inka — Comprehensive museum of Inca and pre-Inca artifacts. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Inca Trail to Machu Picchu (multi-day trek) or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to CUZ for your departure flight.

travelTips: Spend at least one full day in Cusco to acclimatize to the altitude (3,400m) before any strenuous trekking.
Book Machu Picchu entry tickets and the Inca Trail permit months in advance — daily visitor numbers are strictly capped.
Drink coca tea and stay hydrated in the first 48 hours to ease altitude sickness symptoms.
Visit Rainbow Mountain very early morning — afternoon clouds frequently obscure the summit.
The dry season (May-September) is best for trail conditions but also the coldest at night and the most crowded.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Warm layers for cold nights even in the dry season — altitude means big temperature swings
Comfortable, broken-in hiking shoes for Inca Trail or day treks
Coca leaves/tea or altitude medication to help acclimatize
Sun protection — UV is intense at this elevation despite cool air temperatures
thingsToDo: Qorikancha (Temple of the Sun) and Santo Domingo Convent
Inca Trail to Machu Picchu (multi-day trek)
Maras Salt Mines and Moray terraces tour
Tambomachay and Puka Pukara ruins tour
San Pedro Market exterior and stalls
Avenida El Sol colonial architecture walk
Trekking the classic Inca Trail to Machu Picchu
thingsToAvoid: Arriving and immediately attempting strenuous activity — altitude sickness is a real risk without acclimatization.
Booking Inca Trail treks last-minute — permits sell out months ahead, especially June-August.
Underestimating night cold — even in the warm season, temperatures can drop near freezing after dark.
Skipping travel insurance that covers high-altitude trekking and medical evacuation.
Buying "alpaca" goods from unlicensed street vendors — quality and authenticity vary widely versus reputable shops.
topRestaurants: Chicha por Gastón Acurio — Andean-Peruvian Fine Dining ($$$)
MAP Café — Contemporary Peruvian, in the Museo de Arte Precolombino courtyard ($$$$)
Jack's Café Bar — International Comfort Food ($$)
Pachapapa — Traditional Andean, San Blas courtyard ($$)

nightlife: Plaza de Armas bars and clubs

Fine dining tasting menu at MAP Café

Private Sacred Valley picnic among Inca ruins
Luxury train journey through the Andes
Candlelit dinner in a San Blas courtyard restaurant
budgetExperiences: Free wander through San Blas cobblestone streets
Local menú del día lunch for a few dollars near San Pedro Market
Free viewpoint hike to Cristo Blanco
Explore the free Doce Angulos Inca stonework wall
localEtiquette: Altitude sickness is common for new arrivals — locals recommend coca tea (mate de coca) and taking the first day slow.
Quechua culture and Indigenous heritage are central to Cusco's identity — ask permission before photographing local people in traditional dress.
Tipping guides and porters on Inca Trail treks is expected and often a meaningful part of their income.
Bargaining at San Pedro Market or craft stalls is normal and expected, done with good humor.
Many religious and archaeological sites are considered sacred — dress modestly and stay on marked paths.
localTransportGuide: Cusco's historic center is compact and walkable, though steep cobblestone streets and high altitude make it slower going than sea-level cities. Reaching Machu Picchu requires a train from Ollantaytambo or Poroy (no direct road access) followed by a shuttle bus up to the citadel; colectivo vans and organized tour buses cover the Sacred Valley.
bestTimeToVisit: May to September is the dry season with the clearest trail and mountain conditions, though also the busiest and coolest window (chilly nights); shoulder months (April, October) offer fewer crowds.
weather: High-altitude (~3,400m) mountain climate with cool days and cold nights year-round (highs around 19-20°C, dropping toward 17-18°C in the wetter months) and a pronounced dry season (May-September) versus wet season (December-March) with big diurnal temperature swings.
visaInformation: Visa-free entry for most Western passport holders (US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia) for tourist stays up to 90 days, same national entry as Lima. Indian nationals typically need a Peruvian visa arranged in advance; a separate Boleto Turístico (tourist ticket) and Machu Picchu entry permit must be booked ahead regardless of nationality.
averageFlightTime: ~18.2h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Cusco?
faqs.answer: Visa-free entry for most Western passport holders (US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia) for tourist stays up to 90 days, same national entry as Lima. Indian nationals typically need a Peruvian visa arranged in advance; a separate Boleto Turístico (tourist ticket) and Machu Picchu entry permit must be booked ahead regardless of nationality.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Cusco?
faqs.answer: May to September is the dry season with the clearest trail and mountain conditions, though also the busiest and coolest window (chilly nights); shoulder months (April, October) offer fewer crowds. High-altitude (~3,400m) mountain climate with cool days and cold nights year-round (highs around 19-20°C, dropping toward 17-18°C in the wetter months) and a pronounced dry season (May-September) versus wet season (December-March) with big diurnal temperature swings.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Cusco safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Cusco is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Cusco?
faqs.answer: The local currency is PEN. Carry some cash for smaller vendors — card acceptance can be inconsistent outside major hotels and malls.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Anna Cohen
reviews.title: Best vacation we've had
reviews.body: Cusco is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Maras Salt Mines and Moray terraces tour. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Sarah Wang
reviews.title: Best vacation we've had
reviews.body: Cusco is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially San Pedro Market exterior and stalls. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Mohammed Tanaka
reviews.title: Five stars, will return
reviews.body: Sacred Valley full-day tour (Pisac, Ollantaytambo) alone was worth the trip, but combined with Plazoleta de San Blas this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Noah Wang
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: Our trip to Cusco was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Inca Trail to Machu Picchu (multi-day trek) was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Daniel Cohen
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Cusco Cathedral and Cristo Blanco viewpoint. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.
